danger_signals.visual_form: Ghost pirate character model with undead appearance; animated cutscenes showing supernatural manifestation
danger_signals.meaning: Primary antagonist threat; supernatural danger; escalating peril as narrative progresses
LeChuck's power and the supernatural nature of the threat; visual representation of corrupted piracy

danger_signals.visual_form: Combat animation against Carla the Swordmaster; blade clash visuals
danger_signals.meaning: Pirate trial/test; physical danger within game progression

danger_signals.visual_form: Cutscene showing Elaine being taken by LeChuck's crew; island raid animation
danger_signals.meaning: Narrative crisis; stakes escalation; damsel-in-distress scenario

danger_signals.visual_form: LeChuck's ethereal/transparent rendering during final confrontation
danger_signals.meaning: Indicates supernatural nature requiring special countermeasures (voodoo elixir)

safety_signals.visual_form: Colorful character sprites with distinct silhouettes; non-threatening idle animations
safety_signals.meaning: Safe interaction points; sources of information, quests, and puzzle solutions

safety_signals.visual_form: Detailed island backgrounds with navigable paths; point-and-click hotspots
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones for puzzle-solving and resource gathering; no immediate threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Highlighted collectibles (voodoo root, seltzer bottle, root beer); inventory system
safety_signals.meaning: Solutions to progress; safe progression mechanics; player agency in world state change

safety_signals.visual_form: Menu interface with multiple conversation choices presented to player
safety_signals.meaning: Safe information gathering; non-lethal problem-solving approach

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Pirate authority; sword-fighting culture; treasure-seeking ethos
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Pirate hangouts, Carla's sword dojo, buried treasure locations on Mêlée Island

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Skeleton/zombie aesthetic; supernatural piracy; ghost imagery
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Corrupted pirate appearance; skeletal forms; ethereal rendering in ghost state
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: LeChuck's ship; ghost pirate hideout on Monkey Island; supernatural atmosphere
Governor's mansion where valuable idol is housed; church location for wedding

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Tribal identity; voodoo magic practitioners; isolated island culture
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Monkey Island village; dispute with castaway Herman Toothrot; voodoo root source

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Initial state - Guybrush arrives as aspiring pirate
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mêlée Island shown as pirate hub with accessible areas, friendly NPCs, clear quest markers
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs behave normally; island is safe for exploration; trials are presented as achievable challenges

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cutscenes showing LeChuck's ghostly manifestation; undead crew animation; island raid visualization
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPC behavior shifts to show concern; Elaine is abducted; urgency conveyed through narrative pacing

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environment shifts to exotic island with distinct visual palette; cannibal village aesthetics; mysterious atmosphere
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: New faction (cannibals) introduced; Herman Toothrot's isolation shown visually; voodoo magic becomes central mechanic

world_state_to_visual.world_state: LeChuck returns to Mêlée Island for wedding
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Church location added to island; cutscene showing wedding preparation; supernatural aura around LeChuck intensifies
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Elaine shown under duress; Guybrush must act urgently; final confrontation stage set

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ship emporium location becomes combat arena; LeChuck rendered in ghost form; particle effects for elixir impact
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: LeChuck shown as aggressive combatant; Guybrush's actions directly affect ghost pirate vulnerability; root beer substitution resolves threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Explosion animation destroying LeChuck; fireworks celebration; romantic scene with Guybrush and Elaine
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs celebrate; threat eliminated; romantic resolution signals narrative completion

environmental_storytelling.example: Pirate havens on Mêlée Island vs. isolated cannibal village on Monkey Island vs. Governor's mansion - visual hierarchy shows civilization vs. marginalized factions
Meathook with hook hands, Stan the boat salesman in ramshackle shop, Carla as skilled swordmaster - profession and status communicated through sprite design and location
LeChuck's ghost appearance evolves from rumor to visible manifestation; undead crew aesthetic establishes supernatural faction distinctly from living pirates
Voodoo root, seltzer bottle, root beer - magical/mundane items visualized as quest solutions; final substitution mechanic communicated through inventory interface
Story beats (island raid, abduction, wedding, final battle) visualized through animated sequences that raise stakes and shift player perception of world state
Tropical Caribbean island aesthetics for safe zones; darker, more ominous rendering for LeChuck-associated locations; ship interiors show faction identity

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Explores freely, accepts quests, communicates with NPCs; animation reflects youth and determination
Initially described in dialog; later shown in cutscenes with aggressive, commanding presence; ghost form becomes vulnerable to voodoo
Initially flirtatious and independent; later shown as abducted and under duress; final state shows romantic reciprocation after rescue
Demonstrates combat skill during duel; later joins Guybrush's crew as hired companion
Initially presented as potential threat; shift to allies after dispute resolution; trade voodoo root for quest progression
Shows desperation and conflict with cannibals; becomes helpful after intervention
Move in synchronized groups; appear threatening in raid scene; visual representation of supernatural control

notes: The Secret of Monkey Island uses 2D sprite-based visuals to communicate world state through location design, NPC characterization, and narrative cutscenes. The game lacks traditional 'health bars' or danger meters, instead relying on narrative and visual context to convey threat levels. The design philosophy makes player death nearly impossible, shifting focus to puzzle-solving and exploration. Factions are distinguished primarily through visual design (pirate aesthetics vs. supernatural undead vs. tribal cannibals vs. aristocratic governor). World state transitions are explicitly managed through story progression and NPC behavior changes rather than dynamic environmental shifts. The supernatural threat of LeChuck is established through rumor (dialog) before visual manifestation (cutscenes), building narrative tension. The game's humor (root beer as ghost-destroyer) communicates that the pirate world operates by adventure-game logic rather than realistic rules.
